column1: # ZAP scan rules + scope exclusions.
# Format (tab-separated):
#   RULE_ID
column4: NOTE

column1: # Actions: IGNORE, WARN, FAIL
# Apply order: a more-specific URL_REGEX wins. The ACTION on a matched line
# overrides the scan's default severity for that rule on URLs matching the
# regex. Use IGNORE only with a comment line above explaining why.
# Used by both zap-baseline.yml (passive) and zap-scan.yml (active +
# api-scan). The baseline action passes this via `rules_file_name` and the
# full/api actions pass it via `cmd_options: -c .zap/rules.tsv`.
# -- Scope exclusions (Layer 2 from the plan) ---------------------------
# Prevent ZAP from spidering or attacking these — RLS / Supabase platform
# already enforces auth, but exercising them with attack payloads is wasteful
# and noisy. Also keeps `send-email` (HMAC webhook) out of API scan since it
# has no JSON request body shape worth exercising.

column4: Supabase admin API would mutate real users
Storage API — mutating real objects via legitimate JWT
PostgREST — RLS protects rows; iteration is noise
Auth webhook only, HMAC-signed by Supabase

column1: # (Add entries here as `.zap/rules.tsv` is exercised against Dev for the
# first time. Each IGNORE must be preceded by a comment.)
# Proxy Disclosure (40025) — Vercel's CDN exposes `Server: Vercel` and
# the X-Vercel-* family of headers on every response. These cannot be
# stripped without a Vercel Enterprise plan. Accepting the fingerprint
# disclosure on ncp.spej.dev and *.vercel.app preview URLs until/unless
# we upgrade. The underlying mitigations the rule recommends (block
# TRACE/TRACK, custom error pages) are already handled by Vercel's edge.

column4: Vercel CDN platform fingerprint

column1: # Cross-Domain Misconfiguration (10098) on /assets/* — the wildcard
# `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` on bundled JS/CSS is intentional.
# Those files are signed-by-content (SRI integrity hashes via
# vite-plugin-sri3), so the cross-origin readability is safe — the
# browser verifies their integrity against the hash in the parent
# index.html before executing. HTML/dynamic paths are scoped to a
# specific origin via vercel.json.

column4: Intentional wildcard ACAO on SRI-verified bundles

column1: # CORS Misconfiguration (40040) on /assets/* — same root cause as 10098
# above. Different ZAP rule, same intentional behavior.

column4: Intentional wildcard ACAO on SRI-verified bundles

column1: # Sub Resource Integrity (90003) on the Material Symbols CSS link in
# index.html — Google Fonts CSS content varies by User-Agent
# (modern/legacy/woff2/woff), so a static SRI hash is not stable. The
# bundled Vite assets (JS + CSS that we control) DO have integrity
# attributes. To fully clear this we'd need to self-host the Material
# Symbols font, which is tracked as a follow-up. ZAP attaches this
# alert to the parent URL, not the link itself, so the regex matches
# the page URLs.

column4: Google Fonts <link> — UA-dependent CSS, no stable hash

column1: # CSP: style-src 'unsafe-inline' (10055) — Tailwind CSS and Radix UI
# inject inline styles at runtime for theming, dropdowns, dialogs, etc.
# Removing 'unsafe-inline' from style-src would break large parts of
# the SPA. ZAP flags both the wildcard directive and the unsafe-inline
# directive under rule 10055; accepting both as long as the underlying
# component libraries require this pattern. To eliminate properly:
# move to CSS-in-JS with nonces/hashes (significant refactor).

column4: style-src 'unsafe-inline' required by Tailwind + Radix

column1: # Cacheable / Non-Cacheable Content (10049) — ZAP fires this plugin in
# three variants depending on the cache hints in the response:
#   "Storable and Cacheable Content"  → /assets/* (max-age=31536000, immutable)
#   "Storable but Non-Cacheable"      → SPA shell (max-age=0)
#   "Non-Storable Content"            → SPA shell (no-store) + auth-required endpoints
# All three are intentional and informational, not vulnerabilities:
#   - /assets/* are content-hashed SRI-signed bundles, so immutable caching
#     is correct (filename changes when content changes; stale caches are
#     impossible).
#   - The SPA shell uses no-store + max-age=0 so auth bundles aren't cached.
# Widened from the original /assets/* scope to all paths because ZAP's
# Non-Storable variant fires on the shell, which the /assets/* regex
# doesn't match.

column4: Cache policy: immutable on /assets/*, no-store on shell — both intentional

column1: # Retrieved from Cache (10050) — `Age:` response header confirms a CDN
# cache hit on Vercel's edge. Desired behavior for static delivery; the
# only paths that can be cached at the edge are the immutable /assets/*
# bundles (HTML shell is no-store). Not a vulnerability.

column4: Vercel edge CDN cache hit — desired for /assets/* immutable bundles

column1: # Modern Web Application (10109) — descriptive note that the target is a
# JS-heavy SPA. We ARE a React SPA with client-side routing; this is the
# documented architecture. Not a finding, just ZAP characterizing the app.

column4: Target is a React SPA by design; ZAP note is descriptive

column1: # Timestamp Disclosure - Unix (10096) on /assets/*.js — investigated
# 2026-06-05 by grepping the bundle for 10-digit Unix-epoch-range
# numbers. All matches were SHA-1/256/512 round constants (e.g.
# 1859775393, 1732584193 from the FIPS 180-4 hash functions in
# crypto-js) and INT32 boundary constants (2147483647 / -2147483648
# from the Node Buffer polyfill's size validation). These are
# cryptographic primitives and integer limits, not timestamps; ZAP's
# regex pattern is a false positive against minified JS containing
# crypto code.

column4: SHA round constants + INT32 boundaries, not real timestamps

column1: # Information Disclosure - Suspicious Comments (10027) on /assets/*.js
# — investigated 2026-06-05 by grepping the bundle for ZAP's keyword
# list (TODO/FIXME/XXX/HACK/BUG/DEBUG). All matches were code, not
# comments: `oa.debug` property accesses in @supabase/gotrue-js (auth
# log gating), the literal string "Bug : uncompressed data size
# mismatch" inside JSZip's error path, and `debug` as a style key in
# our sonner toast variants. None of these are leaked developer
# annotations — they're functional code and runtime error messages.

column4: Supabase/JSZip identifiers + error strings, not comments

column1: # -- API scan findings on /functions/v1/* (Supabase Edge Functions) ---
# These suppress alerts that fire during the ZAP API scan against our
# Edge Functions on *.supabase.co. Each entry has a comment citing the
# investigation that justified the suppression. The URL_REGEX targets
# the Supabase project domain so these IGNOREs don't leak into the
# full scan against our SPA at ncp.spej.dev.
# Cookie with SameSite Attribute None (10054), Loosely Scoped Cookie
# (90033), Session Management Response Identified (10112), Timestamp
# Disclosure on cookie (10096) — investigated 2026-06-05 by inspecting
# the ZAP evidence. All four fire on the `__cf_bm` Cloudflare bot-
# management cookie (Domain=supabase.co, SameSite=None, value contains
# a Unix timestamp). Supabase routes all traffic through Cloudflare;
# we don't control this cookie's attributes. Not our cookie, not our
# code, not changeable from our side.

column4: Cloudflare __cf_bm cookie (Supabase platform behavior)
Cloudflare __cf_bm cookie scoped to supabase.co
Cloudflare __cf_bm cookie surfaced as session token

column1: # Session Management Response Identified (10112) on Vercel preview URLs
# — fires on the `_vercel_jwt` cookie set by Vercel when the baseline
# workflow's replacer.conf injects `x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie: true`
# (so the deployment-protection bypass persists across the spider/scan
# instead of needing the header on every request). Not our cookie;
# required for the scan to function correctly against protected
# preview deployments. See .github/workflows/zap-baseline.yml.

column4: Vercel _vercel_jwt cookie set by x-vercel-set-bypass-cookie for the scan
Cloudflare __cf_bm cookie value contains a timestamp

column1: # Cross-Domain Misconfiguration (10098) on /functions/v1/* — Edge
# Functions return `Access-Control-Allow-Origin: *` in corsHeaders
# because the SPA runs from multiple deploy URLs (ncp.spej.dev for
# prod, *.vercel.app for previews, dev). Every endpoint requires an
# `Authorization: Bearer <JWT>` header and RLS gates the actual data
# at the database layer, so cross-origin readability of a 401/200
# response doesn't leak anything an attacker couldn't get by making
# the request directly. Tightening to a specific allowlist would
# break preview deployments and provide no additional protection.

column4: Wildcard CORS gated by JWT auth + RLS — explicit accept-risk

column1: # Non-Storable Content (10049) on /functions/v1/* — ZAP flags the
# response as non-storable because the request carried an
# Authorization header. That's the correct behavior: authenticated
# responses MUST NOT be cached by intermediaries. The note is
# descriptive, not a vulnerability.

column4: Auth-required endpoints are correctly non-cacheable

column1: # User Agent Fuzzer (10104) on /functions/v1/* — informational note
# that ZAP fuzzed the User-Agent header to check for UA-based
# response divergence. No divergence found that indicates a
# vulnerability; the responses are gated by JWT, not UA. Not a
# finding.

column4: UA fuzzer informational — auth/RLS doesn't depend on UA

column1: # Unexpected Content-Type was returned (100001) on /functions/v1/* —
# ZAP expected JSON (per the OpenAPI spec) but got text/html in 53
# instances. Investigated 2026-06-05: this happens when ZAP sends
# OPTIONS / GET / malformed POSTs to functions that expect specific
# auth + JSON bodies; the unauthenticated/unrouted path falls back
# to Supabase's gateway HTML error page. Not data we control; the
# functions themselves return JSON on the documented happy path.

column4: HTML error pages from gateway when fuzzer hits unauth/wrong paths

column1: # Server Error 5xx (100000) on /functions/v1/admin-regenerate-backup-codes
# and similar — investigated 2026-06-05: every flagged instance was
# from ZAP's fuzzer sending malformed requests like
# `?class.module.classLoader.DefaultAssertionStatus=nonsense` (a
# Spring4Shell probe). Our Edge Functions throw an unhandled error
# on garbage input rather than returning 4xx because they expect a
# specific POST body shape. The error is contained to the function
# instance; nothing is exposed. Defense-in-depth follow-up would be
# tighter input validation that returns 400 instead of 500, but the
# functional behavior is correct.

column4: Edge Function 500s on fuzzer-malformed input — no data exposure
